items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
id: 7d25c691-18d1-5e39-a40c-a4b7f975abd4
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/16
modified: 2026/06/16
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-16T22:35:46Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:9840136; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-16, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.apex_ai_summary: [P1] Remote Code Execution - critical severity (Risk 9.0/10). [ALERT] CISA KEV CONFIRMED - actively exploited in the wild. Patch immediately. EPSS 0.84% exploitation probability (30-day forecast). MITRE ATT&CK mapped: T1190, T1059 (2 techniques). 1 indicator detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. Actor attribution: CDB-UNATTR-CVE. AI confidence: HIGH (72%) - strong data corroboration.

items.title: Critical Fortinet FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities Actively Exploited in Attacks
items.description:
Threat actors are actively exploiting multiple critical vulnerabilities in Fortinet’s FortiSandbox platform, with live attack telemetry confirming exploitation attempts over the past 24 hours. Defused has flagged three CVEs under active targeting — including one, CVE-2026-39813, with no previously recorded exploitation history. Honeypot sensors and deception infrastructure disguised as Fortinet FortiSandbox instances have captured exploitation […] 
 The post  Critical Fortinet FortiSandbox

items.description: Palo Alto Networks warns that attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2026-0257, a PAN-OS flaw that lets unauthorized users bypass authentication and establish VPN connections. Palo Alto Networks has confirmed active exploitation of CVE-2026-0257, a PAN-OS authentication bypass vulnerability affecting GlobalProtect portals and gateways. Palo Alto Networks addressed the vulnerability on May 13. Two weeks later, cybersecurity firm Rapid7 […]

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — iRhythm Hit by Cyberattack Patient Data Stolen and Ransom Demanded
id: 4c036c43-e920-5736-9645-d4ea0eafb2c7
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to iRhythm Hit by Cyberattack, Patient Data Stolen and Ransom Demanded (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: RANSOMWARE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/iRhythm Hit by Cyberattack, Patient Data Stolen and Ransom Demanded
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/16
modified: 2026/06/16
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_encrypt:
        TargetFilename|endswith:
            - '.locked'
            - '.encrypted'
            - '.crypted'
            - '.enc'
        EventID: 11
    selection_ransomnote:
        TargetFilename|contains:
            - 'README'
            - 'HOW_TO_DECRYPT'
            - 'RECOVER_FILES'
    selection_vssdelete:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'vssadmin delete shadows'
            - 'wmic shadowcopy delete'
            - 'bcdedit /set'
    condition: selection_encrypt or selection_ransomnote or selection_vssdelete
falsepositives:
    - Encryption software (VeraCrypt, BitLocker) in use
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — iRhythm Hit by Cyberattack, Patient Data Stolen and Ransom Demanded — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-16T22:35:46Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "iRhythm Hit by Cyberattack, Patient Data Stolen and Ransom Demanded", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX iRhythm Hit by Cyberattack, Patient Data Stolen and Ransom Demanded Exploitation Attempt - RANSOMWARE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"vssadmin"; nocase; content:"delete shadows"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:1413526; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-16, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.apex_ai_summary: [P1] Ransomware - critical severity (Risk 9.0/10). [ALERT] CISA KEV CONFIRMED - actively exploited in the wild. Patch immediately. EPSS 1.74% exploitation probability (30-day forecast). MITRE ATT&CK mapped: T1486, T1566 (2 techniques). 1 indicator detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. Actor attribution: CDB-UNATTR-RAN. AI confidence: MODERATE (62%) - partial intelligence corroboration.

items.description: Three FortiSandbox flaws, including one patched last week, are being actively exploited, highlighting the shrinking window for defenders.
